original_hypothesis.title: GaugeCL.setInternalBribe Validation Always Passes, Allowing address(0) That Breaks Fee Distribution
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GaugeCL.setInternalBribe uses `require(_int >= address(0), 'zero')` which is always true since address comparisons treat addresses as unsigned integers and the minimum address IS address(0). This allows setting internal_bribe to address(0), which will cause _claimFees to revert when calling safeApprove or notifyRewardAmount on the zero address.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The check `_int >= address(0)` is a demonstrable code defect — it provides zero protection. The developer clearly intended `require(_int != address(0))` or `require(_int > address(0))`. While this requires owner action, it represents incorrect code that could lead to accidental misconfiguration.

proof_of_signal.pattern: require(_int >= address(0), 'zero') — always true; any address passes including address(0)

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: internal_bribe can be set to address(0); _claimFees reverts on IERC20.safeApprove(address(0), ...)

analysis_questions: Can the gauge factory also set internal_bribe to address(0)?
Is there a recovery path if internal_bribe is accidentally set to address(0)?
Does the owner have other ways to fix this (e.g., via GaugeManager.setInternalBribeFor)?

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Owner accidentally or intentionally sets internal_bribe to address(0). All subsequent fee claim attempts for this gauge revert. Fees accumulate in the gauge but cannot be distributed to voters.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Fee distribution permanently blocked for affected gauge until fixed
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Voters of the affected gauge pool

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for setInternalBribe with address(0)

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
